Best Software

Best Plumbing Construction Software (2026)

From emergency service calls to new construction rough-in — plumbing businesses need software that matches how they actually operate.

✓ Updated April 2026

Plumbing businesses often run two very different operations: reactive service work (leaks, clogs, water heater failures) and planned installation work (new construction, bathroom renovations, commercial projects). Software that excels at dispatching technicians for same-day service calls often lacks the project management depth needed for multi-week installation jobs — and vice versa.

This guide covers the best plumbing software by business type, with honest assessments of where each tool shines and where it falls short.

Quick comparison

Software Starting Price Best For Key Strength
ServiceTitan Custom (~$400+/mo) Established plumbing service companies Dispatch, flat-rate pricing, memberships
Jobber $69/mo Small plumbing service businesses Scheduling, quoting, invoicing
Knowify ~$149/mo Commercial plumbing subcontractors AIA billing, job costing
Contractor Foreman $49/mo Small plumbing contractors, mixed work Value, comprehensive feature set
Buildertrend $499/mo Plumbers doing large residential/new construction Full PM, client portal, scheduling

1. ServiceTitan — Best for established plumbing service companies

Pricing: Custom (estimate: $400–$700+/mo for small teams)

ServiceTitan dominates the residential plumbing service market for companies with 5+ technicians. If your business runs on dispatching plumbers to service calls all day, every day — drain cleaning, water heater replacement, leak repair — ServiceTitan's operational tools are unmatched in this category.

Why plumbing service companies use it:

  • Smart dispatch board: Assigns service calls to the right tech based on location, skills, and availability
  • Flat-rate pricing presentation: Techs present tiered options (good/better/best) on a tablet before starting work, increasing average ticket value
  • Maintenance memberships: Sell and manage annual service plan agreements with automatic renewals and scheduling
  • Customer history: Full service history at every property — past repairs, equipment installed, warranties
  • Marketing attribution: Track which lead sources (Google, Angi, radio) are generating the most revenue
  • Financing presentation: Offer financing on large jobs (repiping, water softeners) in the field

Limitations:

  • Expensive and complex — not the right fit for solo operators or small teams
  • Implementation takes weeks; budget for a proper onboarding
  • Not designed for commercial project-based plumbing work

2. Jobber — Best for small plumbing service businesses

Pricing: Core $69/mo · Connect $169/mo · Grow $349/mo

For small plumbing companies under 5 technicians, Jobber gives you the essential workflow tools — quotes, scheduling, job tracking, invoicing — without ServiceTitan's cost and complexity. Many plumbing businesses use Jobber for years until they outgrow it and move to ServiceTitan.

Strengths for small plumbing operations:

  • Online quote requests from your website (customers submit a request, you send a quote)
  • Calendar-based scheduling with mobile access for techs
  • Job notes and photo documentation in the field
  • Automated invoice delivery and payment reminders
  • Credit card processing built in
  • Review request campaigns after job completion

Limitations:

  • No maintenance agreement tracking
  • No flat-rate pricing book
  • Scheduling is simpler than ServiceTitan's intelligent dispatch
  • Not suitable for complex commercial project work

3. Knowify — Best for commercial plumbing subcontractors

Pricing: ~$149/mo starter; contact for Pro and Enterprise tiers

Commercial plumbing subcontractors have completely different software needs than service plumbers. You're managing projects under a GC, tracking costs against a bid, billing with AIA applications for payment, and managing change orders. Knowify was built around this workflow.

Why commercial plumbing subs use it:

  • AIA G702/G703 billing generation — submit proper pay apps to GCs
  • Budget tracking: actual material and labor costs vs. original bid
  • Change order documentation and tracking with approval workflow
  • Project-level job costing that shows true margin
  • QuickBooks integration for seamless accounting

Who this is for: Plumbing companies doing commercial tenant improvements, new construction rough-in and trim-out under a GC, or industrial plumbing installation. Not for service dispatch.

4. Contractor Foreman — Best value for small plumbing contractors

Pricing: Basic $49/mo · Standard $79/mo · Pro $159/mo · Unlimited $299/mo

If you're a small plumbing company doing a mix of service calls and installation work, and budget is a primary concern, Contractor Foreman provides the broadest feature set per dollar. Estimating, scheduling, time tracking, job costing, invoicing, and document management are all included at $49–$79/month. It's not as polished as Jobber or as specialized as Knowify, but it's a legitimate all-in-one option for cost-conscious contractors.

5. Buildertrend — For plumbing in larger residential projects

Pricing: Essential $499/mo · Advanced $799/mo · Complete $1,099/mo

Plumbing contractors who primarily do new residential construction work — rough-in on custom homes, plumbing for large renovations, working as a subcontractor for homebuilders — may find Buildertrend's project management tools valuable. It handles multi-phase scheduling, homebuilder-style workflows, and client communication that other platforms don't address.

Buildertrend — When your plumbing work involves complex projects, not just service calls.

Visit Buildertrend →

Features to prioritize based on your work type

If your business is primarily service and repair

  • Dispatch board with GPS tracking
  • Same-day scheduling capability
  • Flat-rate price book presentation
  • Customer history by service address
  • Maintenance agreement management
  • Online payment collection

If your business is primarily new construction and installation

  • Detailed estimating tied to project phases
  • Job costing (actual vs. budget) in real time
  • Crew scheduling across multiple projects
  • AIA billing (for commercial work)
  • Change order management
  • Integration with QuickBooks or accounting platforms

Quick decision guide

Service-heavy, scaling up → growing team: ServiceTitan

Service-heavy, small team: Jobber

Commercial subcontracting: Knowify

Mixed work on a budget: Contractor Foreman

New residential construction with complex projects: Buildertrend
